title: Global well-posedness for the $L^2$ critical Hartree equation on   $\bbr^n$, $n\ge 3$
comment: Some minor correction on Error estimate made
summary:
We consider the initial value problem for the L^2-critical defocusing Hartree
equation in R^n, n \ge 3. We show that the problem is globally well posed in
H^s(R^n) when 1>s> \frac{2(n-2)}{3n-4}$. We use the "I-method" combined with a
local in time Morawetz estimate for the smoothed out solution.

title: Global regularity of wave maps IV. Absence of stationary or self-similar   solutions in the energy class
comment:
77 pages, no figures. Will not be published in current form, pending
  future reorganisation of the heatwave project
summary:
Using the harmonic map heat flow, we construct an energy class for wave maps
$\phi$ from two-dimensional Minkowski space $\R^{1+2}$ to hyperbolic spaces
$\H^m$, and then show (conditionally on a large data well-posedness claim for
such wave maps) that no stationary, travelling, self-similar, or degenerate
wave maps exist in this energy class. These results form three of the five
claims required in our earlier paper (arXiv:0805.4666) to prove global
regularity for such wave maps. (The conditional claim of large data
well-posedness is one of the remaining claims required in that paper.)

title: Global regularity of wave maps V. Large data local wellposedness and   perturbation theory in the energy class
comment:
73 pages, no figures. Will not be published in current form, pending
  future reorganisation of the heatwave project
summary:
Using the harmonic map heat flow and the function spaces of Tataru and the
author, we establish a large data local well-posedness result in the energy
class for wave maps from two-dimensional Minkowski space $\R^{1+2}$ to
hyperbolic spaces $\H^m$. This is one of the five claims required in an earlier
paper in this series to prove global regularity for such wave maps.

title: Global regularity of wave maps VI. Abstract theory of minimal-energy   blowup solutions
comment:
36 pages, no figures. Will not be published in current form, pending
  future reorganisation of the heatwave project
summary:
In the previous papers in this series, the global regularity conjecture for
wave maps from two-dimensional Minkowski space $\R^{1+2}$ to hyperbolic space
$\H^m$ was reduced to the problem of constructing a minimal-energy blowup
solution which is almost periodic modulo symmetries in the event that the
conjecture fails. In this paper, we show that this problem can be reduced
further, to that of showing that solutions at the critical energy which are
either frequency-delocalised, spatially-dispersed, or spatially-delocalised
have bounded ``entropy''. These latter facts will be demonstrated in the final
paper in this series.

title: Remarks on global a priori estimates for the nonlinear Schr\"odinger   equation
summary:
We present a unified approach for obtaining global a priori estimates for
solutions of nonlinear defocusing Schr\"odinger equations with defocusing
nonlinearities. The estimates are produced by contracting the local momentum
conservation law with appropriate vector fields. The corresponding law is
written for defocusing equations of tensored solutions. In particular, we
obtain a new estimate in two dimensions. We bound the restricted
$L_t^4L_{\gamma}^4$ Strichartz norm of the solution on any curve $\gamma$ in
$\mathbb R^2$. For the specific case of a straight line we upgrade this
estimate to a weighted Strichartz estimate valid in the full plane.

title: The Cauchy problem for the semilinear quintic Schr\"odinger equation in   one dimension,the defocusing case
